content.problemStatement:
You are given a permutation array AAA of integers in the range [1,N][1, N][1,N].

Your task is to handle QQQ queries of the following type:


Given LLL and RRR, first create a new array BBB, also of length NNN, according to the following:

Bi=LB_i = LBi‚Äč=L if Ai<LA_i < LAi‚Äč<L.
Bi=AiB_i = A_iBi‚Äč=Ai‚Äč if L‚Č§Ai‚Č§RL \le A_i \le RL‚Č§Ai‚Äč‚Č§R
Bi=RB_i = RBi‚Äč=R if R<AiR < A_iR<Ai‚Äč.


Find the number of inversions‚Ä†^{\dagger}‚Ä† in BBB.
‚Ä†^{\dagger}‚Ä† An inversion is a pair (i,j)(i, j)(i,j) such that 1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N1 \le i < j \le N1‚Č§i<j‚Č§N and Bi>BjB_i > B_jBi‚Äč>Bj‚Äč.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of multiple lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains NNN and QQQ - the size of the array and the number of queries.
The second line contains NNN integers - A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč, the array.
The next QQQ lines contain 222 integers each - LLL and RRR, the parameters for each query.




Output Format
For each query, output on a new line the number of inversions in the constructed array BBB.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
The sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 2‚čÖ1052 \cdot 10^52‚čÖ105.
